overview:
ImageMagick could be made to crash or run programs as your login if it
opened a specially crafted file.
description:
Benny Isaacs discovered that ImageMagick did not properly manage memory
when processing certain image files. An attacker could possibly use this
issue to cause a denial of service or execute arbitrary code.

 

 

 

Benny Isaacs discovered that ImageMagick did not properly manage memory
when processing certain image files. An attacker could possibly use this
issue to cause a denial of service or execute arbitrary code.
full_text:
Ubuntu Security Notices

USN-8021-1

 

 

 

 

 

 
USN-8021-1: ImageMagick vulnerability

 

 

 

 
Publication date

 

 

 
10 February 2026

 

 

 

 

 
Overview

 

 

 
ImageMagick could be made to crash or run programs as your login if it
opened a specially crafted file.

 

 

 

 

 
Releases

 

 

 

 24.04 LTS

22.04 LTS

20.04 LTS

18.04 LTS

16.04 LTS

14.04 LTS

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Open side navigation
 

 

 
Packages

 

 imagemagick - Image manipulation programs and library
 

 

 
Details

 

 

Benny Isaacs discovered that ImageMagick did not properly manage memory
when processing certain image files. An attacker could possibly use this
issue to cause a denial of service or execute arbitrary code.

 

 

 

Benny Isaacs discovered that ImageMagick did not properly manage memory
when processing certain image files. An attacker could possibly use this
issue to cause a denial of service or execute arbitrary code.

 

 

 

 
Update instructions

 

 

 

 

In general, a standard system update will make all the necessary changes.

 Learn more about how to get the fixes.
 

 

 

 

 
The problem can be corrected by updating your system to the following package versions:

 

 

 

 

 Ubuntu Release
 Package Version
 

 24.04

LTS noble

 imagemagick-6.q16 – 

8:6.9.12.98+dfsg1-5.2ubuntu0.1~esm6

  

Ubuntu Pro
 Fix available with Ubuntu Pro via ESM Apps. A community fix might become publicly available in the future.
